review_policy: Refresh from public official/vendor/standards sources before relying on the catalog for a new matter. Do not include case facts in refresh queries.

sources.source_name: ISO/IEC 27037:2012 Guidelines for identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of digital evidence
sources.status: Published; ISO page states reviewed and confirmed in 2018 and current at catalog review time.
sources.applicable_when: Use as the baseline for identifying, collecting, acquiring, and preserving potential digital evidence across jurisdictions.
sources.why_choose: It is an international baseline for digital evidence handling and cross-jurisdiction exchange.

sources.source_name: ISO/IEC 27041:2015 Guidance on assuring suitability and adequacy of incident investigative method
sources.status: Published; ISO page showed systematic review status at catalog review time.
sources.applicable_when: Use when choosing or justifying methods, tools, validation level, or third-party testing evidence.
sources.why_choose: It anchors the decision that a method must be fit for purpose and supported by validation evidence.

sources.source_name: ISO/IEC 27042:2015 Guidelines for the analysis and interpretation of digital evidence
sources.status: Published; ISO page states reviewed and confirmed in 2021 and current at catalog review time.
sources.applicable_when: Use when analyzing/interpreting artifacts and recording enough information for independent scrutiny.
sources.why_choose: It directly supports reproducible analytical choices and interpretation of digital evidence.

sources.source_name: ISO/IEC 27043:2015 Incident investigation principles and processes
sources.status: Published; ISO page states reviewed and confirmed in 2020 and current at catalog review time.
sources.applicable_when: Use for the overall investigation process from preparation through closure.
sources.why_choose: It supplies the lifecycle frame for why each action is planned, performed, recorded, and closed.

sources.source_name: NISTIR 8387 Digital Evidence Preservation: Considerations for Evidence Handlers
sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.applicable_when: Use for evidence preservation and evidence-management considerations, especially in US-facing work.
sources.why_choose: It is official US government guidance focused on preserving digital evidence and evidence-handler considerations.

sources.source_name: NIST Digital Forensics and Computer Forensics Tool Testing
sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.status: NIST project page active at catalog review time.
sources.applicable_when: Use when choosing, testing, or benchmarking forensic tools against reference datasets.
sources.why_choose: It supports the practice of validating tools and using reference data rather than trusting tooling blindly.

sources.source_name: SWGDE Best Practices for Digital Evidence Collection 18-F-002-2.0
sources.publisher: Scientific Working Group on Digital Evidence
sources.status: Final approved document version 2.0 at catalog review time.
sources.applicable_when: Use for collection planning, documentation, chain of custody, hashing, and live-collection risk decisions.
sources.why_choose: It provides current practitioner best practice and explicitly calls for contemporaneous documentation.

sources.source_name: SWGDE Best Practices for Computer Forensic Acquisitions 17-F-002
sources.publisher: Scientific Working Group on Digital Evidence
sources.status: Referenced by SWGDE collection best-practices page as current acquisition guidance at catalog review time.
sources.applicable_when: Use for acquisition of computers and associated storage media.
sources.why_choose: It is specific to computer forensic acquisition and complements collection guidance.

sources.source_name: Forensic science activities: statutory code of practice - version 2
sources.publisher: UK Forensic Science Regulator and Home Office
sources.applicable_when: Use for England/Wales forensic science quality and statutory-code considerations.
sources.why_choose: It is the current statutory quality-code source for forensic science activities in England and Wales.

sources.source_name: ACPO Good Practice Guide for Digital Evidence, version 5
sources.status: Legacy guide listed as current by NBS at catalog review time; prefer UK FSR Code for current statutory quality requirements.
sources.applicable_when: Use only when local SOPs, legal teams, or jurisdictional practice still reference the ACPO principles.
sources.why_choose: It remains widely referenced for the classic UK digital evidence principles, especially audit trail and competent-person accountability.

sources.source_name: ENFSI Best Practice Manual for Digital Forensic Examination
sources.publisher: European Network of Forensic Science Institutes
sources.status: Public ENFSI best-practice manual source at catalog review time.
sources.applicable_when: Use for European lab/examination quality framing and validation expectations.
sources.why_choose: It gives a European forensic-science institute perspective for method validation and quality controls.

sources.status: AFP publication page active at catalog review time.
sources.applicable_when: Use for Australian government investigation decision records, legal adherence, evidence continuity, and exhibit handling.
sources.why_choose: It is current Australian government investigation guidance and explicitly requires decision context, rationale, maker, date, and implementation details.
